title: Base Station Operation Log
description: Completeness and power certification for a base station recording session. Consumed by base_station_score -> Data Completeness & Integrity building block (Stage 1, PPK workflow). Captures ONLY what RINEX cannot self-report: clean termination, power state, and an end-time cross-check. Does NOT duplicate RINEX timestamps, gaps, epoch counts, or signal quality.

session_completed_normally.description: true only if recording was stopped by deliberate operator action (stop button / normal shutdown). false if it ended any other way. null = device did not report (scored as unconfirmed, not failed). Single most important field.

unexpected_shutdown_count.description: Count of unexpected shutdowns/resets DURING the session (recommended: mid-session only, not the final fatal shutdown). 0 = healthy. >=1 drives a flag. null = unreported.

battery_start_pct.description: Battery level at session start (0-100). null for mains/solar units with no internal battery (scored not-applicable).

battery_end_pct.description: Battery level at session end (0-100). null for mains/solar units or if device died before writing.

battery_min_pct.description: Lowest battery level reached during the session (0-100). More important than the endpoints: a session that dipped to 3% mid-flight was at risk even if it recovered. null = not applicable/unreported.
battery_min_pct.x-on-low: Threshold ~<=10% -> risk penalty even if session completed

session_end_utc.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of when the DEVICE believes the session ended. Used as a cross-check only against the RINEX last-observation time, never as a primary timestamp. null = unreported.
session_end_utc.x-cross-check: Compared to RINEX last epoch; disagreement beyond tolerance -> FLAG: BASE_RINEX_TRUNCATED

raw_log_download_confirmed.description: OPTIONAL but recommended. true once the raw observation (RINEX) file has actually been transferred off the device. Closes problem #8 (log stranded on device). Do not block the required fields on this.

x-nullability-rule: All fields are nullable. null means 'device did not report this' and is scored as UNCONFIRMED, which is deliberately distinct from a bad value. Never substitute a default (do not write 0 or 100 for unknown battery). This lets the score distinguish known-bad from unknown.
x-scope-discipline: Capture ONLY completeness and power certification. Do not add recording start/end as primary timestamps, gap detection, epoch counts, satellite counts, or signal quality. If a proposed field can be answered from RINEX, it does not belong here.

x-edge-cases.abrupt_total_power_loss: Define whether session_completed_normally is written false, or the entire file is absent. Both acceptable; we must know which.
x-edge-cases.shutdown_count_scope: Recommend counting only mid-session recoverable events; let session_completed_normally=false carry the 'ended badly' signal.
x-edge-cases.mains_or_solar_unit: All three battery fields must be null (not 0, not 100) so the score reads battery not-applicable.

examples._label: Power interruption during session (finished, but at risk)
Silent truncation (device died, file written by recovery path)
